fresh_facts.claim: US Navy forces seized an Iranian-flagged cargo ship attempting to evade a US naval blockade of Iranian ports over the weekend of April 19-20, 2026.
fresh_facts.source: Al Jazeera, Times of India, CGTN, Arab News

fresh_facts.claim: A US destroyer reportedly fired at the Iranian-flagged vessel on April 20, 2026 at 00:29 UTC.
Iran's IRGC and joint military command declared the Strait of Hormuz closed until the US lifts its blockade on April 19, 2026 at 00:29 UTC.
Iran reportedly conducted drone operations targeting US naval assets in retaliation for the ship seizure, with Iranian sources claiming a strike on a US ship though details remain unconfirmed.
President Trump maintained the blockade of Iran 'in full force' and threatened to destroy Iran's power plants and bridges if no nuclear deal is reached.
Iran's Foreign Ministry rejected plans for second-round talks in Islamabad, citing US ceasefire violations including the ship seizure and blockade.
A two-week ceasefire between the US and Iran expires on April 21, 2026.
Iran's President accused the US of denying Iran's nuclear rights on April 20, 2026 at 00:27 UTC.
Pakistan continues mediation efforts for talks scheduled for April 21-22, 2026 despite Iran's rejection signals and intensified contacts with both sides.
Iranian Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf criticized hardline opponents of potential US talks as 'extremist' actors undermining negotiations.
An Iranian lawmaker stated that enriched uranium must not leave the country and warned that talks under threats would 'embolden the enemy.'
Iran's Foreign Minister informed Russia's Lavrov that US ceasefire breaches and a ship seizure have damaged diplomacy.
The UN nuclear watchdog chief stated that an Iran deal without oversight is 'an illusion.'
The IRGC fired on a tanker in the Strait of Hormuz on Saturday, April 18, 2026 as posturing to strengthen Iran's position ahead of diplomacy.
Iran reimposed the Hormuz blockade just a day after announcing its reopening, signaling strategic reversals possibly linked to Lebanon ceasefire demands.
100 days after January 2026 protests, Iran's economy faces severe inflation, unemployment, and war damage.
The Iranian government retreated from promises to compensate homeowners hit by US-Israeli strikes, sparking resident anger.
Iran's Supreme National Security Council approved selective global internet access for businesses via 'internet pro' to sustain economic activity.
The US extradited an Iranian from Panama on sanctions charges.
January 2026 saw one of the most widespread and violent protest waves in Iran, with public mood remaining fractured 100 days later between grief, uncertainty, and fear.

regime_fall.current_status: Iran faces an imminent ceasefire expiration on April 21, 2026 amid escalating military confrontations—US naval seizures and blockade enforcement clashing with Iranian Strait of Hormuz closures and drone retaliations—while diplomatic channels collapse as Iran rejects talks citing ceasefire violations, and internal divisions emerge between negotiation-willing officials and hardline opponents.
regime_fall.last_major_event: US Navy seized an Iranian-flagged cargo ship attempting to evade the US naval blockade on April 19-20, 2026, prompting Iran's IRGC to declare the Strait of Hormuz closed and reject planned second-round talks in Islamabad, while Trump threatened to destroy Iranian infrastructure if nuclear demands are not met before ceasefire expiry.
